title: Fruits Into Baskets
intuition: We need the longest subarray containing at most 2 distinct elements. This is a classic sliding window problem where we expand the window and shrink it when the condition (<=2 distinct fruits) is violated.
approach: Brute: Generate all subarrays and check distinct count. Better: Use set/map for each starting point. Optimal: Use sliding window with hashmap to track counts and maintain at most 2 distinct fruits.
complexity analysis: Brute: O(N^3), Better: O(N^2), Optimal: O(N)
Pattern: Variable Size Sliding Window with condition (at most K distinct elements)
real world use: Used in streaming systems where you need to find longest continuous segment with limited categories (e.g., max 2 product types in a cart, or limited resource usage windows).

title: Longest Repeating Character Replacement
intuition: We want the longest substring where we can replace at most k characters to make all characters same. Key idea: Track the most frequent character in the window. If window size - maxFreq > k, shrink window.
approach: Brute: Try all substrings and check replacements needed. Better: Use frequency array for each substring. Optimal: Sliding window maintaining max frequency character and shrinking when replacements exceed k.
complexity analysis: Brute: O(N^3), Better: O(N^2), Optimal: O(N)
Pattern: Variable Size Sliding Window with constraint (window size - maxFreq <= k)
real world use: Useful in error-tolerant systems like signal processing or typo-tolerant search where limited corrections are allowed to maintain consistency.
